title: BenchmarkRegistration
description: Set 069 S5 - the PRE-REGISTERED benchmark for the measured replacement gate. Written as benchmark-registration.json BEFORE the automated surface is scored, so the pass thresholds cannot be moved to fit the result. Mixes seeded defect classes with holdout cases (recent real misses, e.g. the two Major bugs the 0.22.0 automated run missed) so the benchmark measures the actual manual-vs-automated gap. minCasesForPower records the n below which a result is reported underpowered rather than silently passed. The runtime contract is the pure-Python validator ai_router.replacement_gate.validate_benchmark_registration; ai_router.replacement_gate.score_benchmark derives recall/precision/replay-success/false-REPRODUCED and the cadence recommendation. See docs/proposals/2026-06-16-pull-architecture-capabilities/proposal.md Sec.1.6.

name.description: A stable benchmark name. The scoreboard's benchmarkName must match it (identity).

minCasesForPower.description: The minimum real-case count for a powered result. Below this, score_benchmark reports underpowered=true and meets_thresholds is forced False. Default 10.

thresholds.description: The pass bar, committed before scoring. recall/precision/replaySuccess are minimums; maxFalseReproducedRate is a maximum. All are numbers in [0, 1].

cases.description: The benchmark cases (real defects the surface should catch). At least one, and at least one of kind 'holdout'.

kind.description: 'seeded' = a known/injected defect class; 'holdout' = a recent real miss (the gap the benchmark must measure).

probeable.description: Whether a deterministic probe can falsify this class (advisory).

sourceRef.description: For a holdout case, the real miss it came from (e.g. a commit / release / incident ref).
